pi_items.source: create_ecoli_classic.ps1 lines 92-106 INSERT statement only extracts HAMBURGER from [fh2].[Beefjerkey]. No extraction code found for the other 11 claimed variables (HOTDOG, WATERMELON, LETTUCE, MUSTARD, RELISH, KETCHUP, ONION, PEPPERS, CORN, TOMATO, GROUNDMEAT)

pi_items.source: setup_task.ps1 line 88 contains hard-coded statement 'Table: FoodHistory (359 records)' but this is not derived from querying the actual database

pi_items.metadata_value: Real CDC E. coli O157:H7 outbreak investigation data bundled with Epi Info 7
pi_items.verified_value: Epi Info sample datasets are teaching/training datasets, not real outbreak data
pi_items.source: Web searches on Epi Info documentation and tutorials indicate sample datasets are synthetic teaching datasets. Search results show Epi Info tutorials use fictional foodborne outbreak investigations for training purposes. The README claims real CDC data but this conflicts with published information about Epi Info sample projects.

pi_items.verified_value: Unknown - no specific picnic outbreak with 359 records and these food variables found
pi_items.source: Extensive web searches found: 1) 1992-1993 Jack in the Box E. coli outbreak (hamburger-only, 500+ cases, not a picnic). 2) Oswego outbreak (church supper not picnic, Staph aureus not E. coli, 75 people not 359). No documented picnic E. coli outbreak with exactly 359 records and the specified 12 food variables was found.

privileged_info_summary: The EColi.mdb dataset contains a FoodHistory table with verified variables ILLDUM (outcome), ONSETDATE, SEX, and AGENUM (age). The HAMBURGER food exposure variable is confirmed. However, the metadata claims 12 food exposure variables (HAMBURGER, HOTDOG, WATERMELON, LETTUCE, MUSTARD, RELISH, KETCHUP, ONION, PEPPERS, CORN, TOMATO, GROUNDMEAT) but the database extraction script only extracts HAMBURGER; no extraction code was found for the other 11 claimed variables. The record count of 359 is stated in setup scripts but not verified from actual data. Critically, the metadata claims 'Real CDC E. coli O157:H7 outbreak investigation data' but Epi Info sample datasets are known to be synthetic teaching datasets rather than real CDC outbreak data.
